title: Z-SSWS × Z-Lab unified Cursor project control spine
governance_note: Registry is declarative posture only. No automated side effects are executed from this JSON.

z_ssws.role_summary: Main control spine for all Cursor IDE project folders under the hub and PC root policy.
responsibilities: Primary cockpit: unified dashboards, operator surfaces, and controlled doorways into workspaces and sibling projects
Authoritative project registry alignment (canonical paths, manifests, EAII-facing roster pointers where applicable)
Readiness routing: status gates (GREEN/YELLOW/BLUE/RED/NAS_WAIT) before operator or scripted open actions
Doorway opening: governed local launcher only (Cursor/VS Code/Explorer) — never deploy or run package lifecycles from spine scripts
Mini-bot coordination: traffic/comms/status signals as read-only or explicitly human-triggered lanes; no silent autonomy loops
out_of_scope_for_this_phase: Auto-deploy, secret writes, background service mutation
NAS mutation; Synology posture remains declaration-only until mount verification

z_lab.role_summary: Supervised background analysis and build-readiness layer — observation and recommendations, not autonomous execution.
responsibilities: Background analysis: lint-like aggregates, structure/registry checks, and read-only similarity or cadence reports where scripted
Dry-run checks: verification commands and report emission without changing remote or production state
Report generation: markdown/JSON under data/reports with human-readable operator summaries
Suggested Cursor prompts: templates and slash-command hints stored as docs or data; no auto-submission to AI
Build-readiness scoring: advisory numeric or tier labels; does not flip production or merge branches
out_of_scope_for_this_phase: Auto-run dev servers or wrangler from Z-Lab scripts without explicit operator invocation
Writing to NAS volumes before Synology mount and policy sign-off

status_semantics.GREEN: Safe to proceed with the described local/supervised action (open doorways, run verify, review dashboards).
status_semantics.YELLOW: Proceed with conscious review; may open folders or run verifiers after operator acknowledges warning context.
status_semantics.BLUE: Hold: AMK or governance decision required before expanding scope or binding external surfaces.
status_semantics.RED: Blocked: do not perform the gated action; fix posture or governance first.
status_semantics.NAS_WAIT: Wait for Synology/NAS mount and verification; treat NAS paths as declaration-only until present on disk.

rules: Registry and docs may reference future Z: or UNC paths as intent only
No hub script may create, delete, or sync content on NAS until mount presence and human verification are confirmed
NAS_WAIT status applies to any path that requires the Synology volume before IDE open or verify
